title: OpenWeather API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: OpenWeather API key detected (32-character hexadecimal format). OpenWeather API keys provide access to weather data services including current weather, forecasts, historical data, and meteorological information. Exposing these keys enables unauthorized API usage, consuming your quota, accessing weather data at your expense, and potentially exhausting rate limits. Revoke this key immediately from openweathermap.org and use environment variables or secret management systems for secure storage.

OpenWeather API key detected in variable assignment. OpenWeather API keys authenticate access to weather data APIs for current conditions, forecasts, historical weather, and climate information. Exposing these keys in code allows unauthorized users to consume your API quota, access weather data services, and incur costs from premium API tiers. Use secure credential management and rotate keys immediately if exposed.

OpenWeather API key detected in appid parameter. OpenWeather uses 'appid' as the API key parameter name in HTTP requests. Exposing this parameter with hardcoded keys enables unauthorized access to weather data services, consuming API quotas and incurring costs. Store API keys securely in environment variables and implement proper access controls.

Generic OpenWeather API key detected in source code (32-character alphanumeric format). OpenWeather API keys authenticate access to weather data services including current weather conditions, 5-day/3-hour forecasts, historical weather data, weather maps, and air pollution information. Exposing these credentials enables unauthorized API usage, financial losses from premium tier consumption, exhaustion of rate limits, and potential service disruption. Use environment variables or secret managers for secure credential storage and rotate keys immediately.
